entry.title: Re: Can the service bundle be pre-configured with specifi...
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, in the Service scope 3D2 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, it is possible to predefine the materials for service bundles. This ensures that users do not need to manually create material entries each time a service bundle is selected. Service bundles consist of a main item and one or more subitems, which can include service products, service parts, and expense items. Service - Service Bundles The service bundle can be configured to automatically include the predefined materials as part of the subitems linked to the main bundle item. This setup not only saves time but also standardizes the offerings provided to customers and aids in quick service processing. More detailed configuration and handling of service bundles can be found in the specific documentation on service bundles.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Populate default value in custom field added in Servi...
entry.content:
Hello,

To default a custom field value in the service order header and ensure it's populated during editing, you need to modify your implementation of the CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY BAdI. Here's how to address the issue:

  1. Correct the BAdI implementation:

    • Remove the statement serviceheader_extension_out = serviceheader_extension_in from your BAdI implementation .
    • This statement overwrites your new values with the old ones, preventing your custom logic from taking effect.

  2. Implement the correct logic:

    • In the BAdI method, assign your desired default value directly to the corresponding field in the serviceheader_extension_out structure .

  3. Ensure the BAdI is called during editing:

    • The BAdI should be triggered both when creating and editing service orders.
    • Double-check your BAdI implementation to ensure it's properly registered and activated.

  4. Consider using the SERVICE_TRANSACTION_CHECK BAdI:

    • If the CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY BAdI is still not triggered during editing, you may need to use the SERVICE_TRANSACTION_CHECK BAdI.
    • This BAdI allows you to check and modify service order data before saving.

  5. Verify custom field properties:

    • Ensure that the custom field properties are set correctly in the BAdI implementation.
    • The CRMS4_SERV_CUST_H_MODIFY BAdI is specifically designed to set field properties for extension fields at the solution order header level.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Compounded key value in AFO BOBJ report output

Hi Experts,
We use BOBJ reports based on AFO(Analysis for Office) and these reports are based on CDS views in SAP. We have recently upgraded our S4 system to 2023 version and post upgrade, we started seeing compounded values for some of the fields in the BOBJ output.

Thank you,

Amarendra.

entry.title: Enhancing Data Integration and Collaboration through Asset Administration Shell in SAP BNAC
entry.content:
What is SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC)?

 SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC) solution offers a common foundation for collaborative asset management across different stakeholders involved in the lifecycle of physical assets—such as machinery, equipment, or infrastructure. By utilizing digital twins of these assets, SAP BNAC enhances collaboration among component suppliers, OEMs, and asset operators. This improves resilience, transparency, and operational reliability throughout the entire asset lifecycle. It also extends SAP S/4HANA for B2B integration of asset data and maintenance, using features from SAP Intelligent Asset Management and SAP Fieldglass solutions.

More information about SAP BNAC can be found here.

 

What are Digital Twins and why we need standards?

Simply speaking, digital twins can be seem as virtual representations of physical assets, systems, or processes. By mimicking real characteristics and behaviors of its physical counterparts during its lifecycle, digital twins provide numerous opportunities for business to provide insights into performance, maintenance needs, and enhance overall efficiency.

To fully exploit the potential of digital twins, data is collected from a variety of sensors and data sources with its own formats and data models. While this process partly occurs within the boundaries of a single company, it often involves numerous stakeholders throughout the product lifecycle. In this context, standards are crucial for digital twins to ensure consistency, interoperability, and seamless integration across different platforms and industries. This is where Asset Administration Shell (AAS) becomes significant.

 

Introducing Asset Administration Shell (AAS)

The Asset Administration Shell (AAS) is an industry standard (IEC 63278) for digital twins and driven by industrial leaders under the Industrial Digital Twin Association (IDTA). It provides standardized meta model and APIs allowing both unambiguous identification, representation of physical assets as well as interoperable integration to systems (data sources) relevant for digital twins. One of the features of the AAS is the concept of Submodels. Submodels organizes digital twin data following a specific use case or domain. These Submodels can adhere to established standards such as ECLASS and VDI 2770, ensuring interoperability across different systems, allowing for the seamless sharing and interaction of digital twin data. Well known examples of Submodels are Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation.

Example of an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) of a robot with Submodels.

  Figure 1 Example of an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) of a robot with Submodels.

 

Asset Administration Shell (AAS) support in SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC)

By providing support to the AAS, SAP BNAC establishes an interoperability foundation for component suppliers, OEMs, and operators, facilitating the use of digital twins in collaboration processes. The new feature will enable users to utilize AAS to create corresponding business objects in SAP BNAC and integrate them into business processes via SAP S/4HANA.

High-level overview of collaborative asset management leveraging AAS.

 Figure 2 High-level overview of collaborative asset management leveraging AAS.

  • Seamless and Interoperable Data Import:
    SAP BNAC now supports the effortless import of AAS files (AASX) through its user interface. This allows for the integration of Submodels like the Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation to create Equipment objects. This standardization simplifies data handling and ensures consistency.
  • Integration with SAP S/4HANA:
    The AAS import functionality translates AAS data into standard SAP objects, integrating them into SAP Plant Maintenance and SAP Material Master. This integration ensures that asset data is not only accurate but also easily accessible across the SAP ecosystem.
  • Extended Partner Collaboration:
    SAP BNAC extends its collaborative capabilities beyond the SAP ecosystem. It ensures interoperability with data ecosystems like Factory-X, enabling businesses to engage with a broader network of partners and service providers.

Figure 2 shows the use case leveraging AAS for asset management with SAP BNAC. In this use case, AAS can be used to transport data of digital twins from other data sources (e.g., engineering system, manufacturing system, etc.) into SAP BNAC. Alternatively, the user could also obtain the AAS from a supplier. With the new feature, SAP BNAC creates the corresponding business object by importing the AAS and allows their integration into SAP S/4HANA. Once the business objects are created, the user can share them with their business partners using SAP BNAC.

A robot manufacturer provides a digital twin of their products to their customers (operators). Typically, the manufacturer spends considerable time searching for component data from suppliers and manually maintaining this information in their SAP system. With SAP BNAC's new functionality, manufacturers can automatically create an Equipment with corresponding Documents by importing an AAS Instance that includes Submodels such as Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation. The manufacturer can then use existing SAP BNAC features to share the Equipment and Documents with their customer (operator). The operator can integrate the received Equipment and Documents into their SAP S/4HANA system, facilitating additional business processes like maintenance planning.

 

Begin Your Journey in Standardized Digital Twins with the SAP BNAC 2504 Release

Starting with 2504 release, SAP BNAC will enable the import of an AAS file (AASX) containing an AAS Instance with Submodel Digital Nameplate and Handover Documentation through the user interface to create Equipment objects and the corresponding Documents.

Learn more about this feature: https://url.sap/x7hntw

Below are some of screenshots showing the new release. With the feature “Data Import” (Figure 4-7), users can now upload an AASX file (as shown Figure 3). Figure 8-10 shows the resulting Equipment and its Document as derived from AAS.

Figure 3 AASX file with the AAS Instance of a Safety Valve from LESER as modeled with Eclipse AASX Package Explorer from IDTA.

Figure 4 SAP BNAC initial UI with Data Import Feature highlighted.

Figure 5 AASX Import - Part 1: User click on "New" to import AAX File.

Figure 6 AASX Import - Part 2: Users select corresponding AASX file from AAS Instance of LESER Safety Valve as shown above.

Figure 7 AASX Import - Part 3: SAP BNAC processes the AASX file and generates the corresponding Equipment, Attributes and Documents accordingly.

Figure 8 AASX Import – Part 4: Resulting Equipment as derived from AASX file.

Figure 9 AASX Import - Part 5: Equipment Data Sheet containing the Attributes as derived form AAS Submodel Digital Nameplate.

Figure 10 AASX Import - Part 6: Documents derived from AAS Submodel Handover Documentation.

 

Conclusion

The 2504 release of SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC) marks a significant milestone in the realm of collaborative asset management. By supporting Asset Administration Shell (AAS), SAP BNAC offers an effortless and standardized way to import, integrate, and manage asset data as digital twins. This enhancement ensures that businesses can capitalize on the full potential of digital twins, fostering a more collaborative and efficient environment. Here is an overview of the value proposition:

  • Automatic Asset Data Creation:  Saves time and money by avoiding manual errors.
  • Easy Data Sharing: Allows manufacturers, operators, and service providers to focus on their business processes.
  • Integrate industry standards into your business processes: Synchronize digital twins with SAP S/4HANA (Plant Maintenance and Material Master).
  • Future-proof: Getting ready for Digital Product Passport (DPP) and collaborating in Factory-X.

 

Join us and be part of the Digital Twin Ecosystem

We are pleased to introduce this innovative feature and invite you to assist us in refining and perfecting it. Your feedback is invaluable as we enhance the AAS support in SAP BNAC. Join us in driving the future of digital twin technology.

Please contact us today (email ) to learn how SAP BNAC can transform your business operations.

At SAP, we are dedicated to more than just software development—we are pioneering the next wave of industrial innovation. Harness the power of digital twins to elevate your business to new heights.

This is only the beginning—additional enhancements are forthcoming as we continue to broaden our support for industry standards and cross-system connectivity. Stay tuned!

Further information:

  • SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration
  • SAP Help Portal - What's New in SAP BNAC
  • SAP Help Porta - Data Import (AAS Import)

entry.title: Project Kernseife - improving the measurement of Clean Core in ABAP
entry.content:
TL;DR

To improve the handling and measuring of classic ABAP code in ATC with regards to clean core, the possibility of an ATC check based on a custom classification was developed and is now published as an open source project named "Project Kernseife".

Challenges in Measuring Clean Core

Working in the area of clean core - especially focusing on the dimension "extensibility" - in projects of different scales, we were more than once asked in the last years on how to measure and govern the clean core principles within SAP S/4HANA systems (focusing on Private Cloud & On-Premises).

A key part to describe the "clean core state" of extensions is from our point of view the creation of a technical debt score that should align with the governance process in your system. That is why SAP's own code quality tool ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) is very helpful on this. Since the release of "ABAP Cloud" as target for On-Stack extensions, we have now possibibilities with the related ATC Check Variant (ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS) to identify the "distance to Tier 1".

But looking at landscapes with large amount of legacy code (e.g. > 100,000 custom code objects in classic ABAP) or systems where you cannot use sufficiently released APIs for Tier 1, it becomes more difficult to identify the "real" technical debt.

If you run with the default ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS variant into larger systems, you might be overwhelmed by a huge number of findings in ATC - that are all marked as Error. This is logical, if you want to identify the criticality as "what do I need to change to get into ABAP Cloud", but at the same time the important technical debt might be hidden behind a lot of "false positive" findings.

To improve this situation, SAP released the new 3 Tier check (SAP Note 3565942) in February of 2025. This check improves the situation by analyzing "classic" coding with a stronger focus on technical debt, to identify the "right" things. It is based on SAP's released objects (Tier 1, official APIs) and nominated Classic APIs. These Classic APIs belong either to business logic (e.g. BAPI_PO_CREATE1) or a technical framework (e.g. ALV). Finally, there are now also "noAPIs" (objects that must not be used, e.g. RFC_READ_TABLE) nominated by SAP.

Still - we saw a gap in projects to improve the lifes of lead developers: A custom classification. Because SAP may not be able to classify a certain ABAP object as precise as you may be able to do. So SAP is not publishing any information on a given ABAP object and it is shown as accessing an internal object (which leads in ATC to a warning). But in your use case, you are fine with using it. And this is where Kernseife can help you.

What is "Kernseife"?

Kernseife consists of two parts:

  1. A custom ATC Check that is based on the new Tier 3 check mentioned above, but uses a custom classification system.
  2. CAP Application to create a custom classification of ABAP objects (and we even use it to analyze results from systems in a simple way).

ATC Check for custom classification

As mentioned, we started to work on "Kernseife" in 2024 as there was no detailed Tier 3 check available then. Therefore, our focus was initially to create a custom ATC check that can work with a custom classification. It is now re-worked and based on the "new" ATC Check (Note 3565942).

This check is basically analyzing your custom code like the Tier 3 check, but when it comes to the evaluation of the impact of a finding, the custom classification comes into play. You can upload the custom classification as a JSON file and in this classification ywill then used to determine your findings. Let's explain the difference with an example:

In your project, you need to work with the RF framework, because you are implementing EWM in your Private Cloud Environment. Therefore, you need to work with the classes /SCWM/CL_RF*, that are the recommended way to interact with the RF framework. If you look into the Cloudifcation Repostory, you can see that these classes are not released or nominated as "classic API". Still, you want to generally accept the usage of the RF framework for both: Governance of developers (ATC Checks) and measuring your "distance to clean core".

By using your custom classification which contains the classes /SCWM/CL_RF* as "Business Function - Recommended" (BF1), which will lead to only "Information" (Prio 3) findings in the check and will have a lower impact on the "clean core score", you can improve your efficiency.

For details on the custom classification, you can check the classification guide.

Application to manage Classifications

Of course, you could maintain the JSON file of the classification manually based on the given json format. But we created a little app, that is currently in a feature branch, to do that in a more sophisticated way.

In this application, you can initially check the current state for objects in as defined by SAP and additionally add objects that or not yet mentioned by SAP.

Sticking with the example from above, you can now edit the "Rating Code" of the /SCWM/CL_RF* classes to "BF1". After you did your changes, you can download the classification JSON file, upload it into your ATC check system and all of the findings for these classes will in future be recorded as "Information" (Prio 3) message.

Governance and Measurability

With this custom classification and the usage in ABAP Test Cockpit, we can now combine the governance view and the measurability within ATC.

Governance

By enhancing SAP's default rating, you can control your developers more efficiently. Because as of today, your lead developers that are able to approve exemptions might be overwhelmed by a lot of "false positive" ATC findings. Just imagine every usage of the example classes "/SCWM/CL_RF*" need to be exempted.

In projects - especially looking at the overwhelming errors from the "ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS" ATC check in classic ABAP, you can now control with your custom classification, that recurring "false positive" findings will disappear (or become prio 3 messages only).

This is important, as - being a reviewer - your chances to ask questions on the important findings will improve. Because if for every major developments you need to approve hundreds of exemption requests, you will most probably go into "autopilot" approval mode as you cannot check all of the requests in detail.

Measurability

Our second motivation to create this check is to create a more realistic impact rating to measure what we call the "Kernseife Score" (basically: a technical debt score per object). With every classification (BF1), we consider a score of how severe the impact of using this SAP object is with regards to clean core.

So you can configure now a score - we did use a 0 to 10 scale - to identify how severe is a single finding. Based on the ATC check, we can now provide per object a score that you can use for your reporting. Either to identify the total score per system, per package, per object - or even to deep dive into analyses like "which object has the worst average score per finding?" or "which object is in total view the "uncleanest" object in my system?".

While you can then easily use your analytics tools (e.g. SAP Analytics Cloud) to create the dashboards you need, in our repository you can at least find a CDS-View you can use for extraction (ZKNSF_I_SCORING), that combines the findings with the score. If you want to directly extract e.g. a list aggregated by object, you could directly use another CDS-View (ZKNSF_I_DEVELOPMENT_OBJECTS).

What's next?

Kernseife is currently in use (or in prior versions) in several projects. But to be very clear: There is development ongoing, but we do not have a product roadmap available.

But as it is open source, we are open to feature discussions or pull requests - or just use it for your own advantage!

entry.title: Sending multiple sales / billing documents in one e-mail
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there any way to consolidate multiple Order confirmations or Billing documents into one e-mail?

We could have multiple sales order created for the same customer the same day and the customer would appreciate only getting one e-mail with all order confirmations instead of being bombarded with e-mails with order confirmations. 

Same thing with Billing document. We invoice the same customer multiple orders the same day. We need to have separate invoices. But the customer would appreciate to get one e-mail with all invoices attachements instead of 100 separate e-mails.

Kind Regards,

Cecilia

entry.title: Re: Service Quotations (Requested Start Date - End Date)
entry.content:
Hello,

Reasons for Date Adjustments

End Date Change

The automatic adjustment of the end date could be linked to system configurations or custom logic implemented via SAP’s framework. For instance, Configurable Parameter and Formula (CPF) framework allows Flexible Proposal of Requested Delivery Dates on Sales Order for dynamic adjustments based on rules defined by your system administrators source.

Posting Date Delay

The discrepancy in the posting date can arise due to standard system behavior using system dates Sales Order change/create times are unclear instead of user-entered dates. This might be influenced by system time settings, reflecting transactions based on server dates rather than those set by users source.

These adjustments are generally in place to accommodate business processes and ensure operational consistency within workflows.

The service may have lead time, the lead time is considered and may affect your end date.

Recommendations

  • Check Configuration: Review any custom settings or rules associated with service quotation dates to ensure they are set as intended.
  • Consult Documentation: If CPF rules might be affecting date results, consulting documentation or your system administrator for changes could be a solution.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

What My goal would be is to launch process chains based on the values of the row selected.

Problem is I've stucked on the 0. th step. 


I've created the action button in the metadata extension and implemented the launchProcess method to just launch one specific processchain only as a first step.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG Parallel processing in Mass processing of data
entry.content:
Hi

The Consolidation and Mass Processing has been build to handle data at object level and on activation of the request, it is looking to lock the entire object into the CR. Hence any Change Request Type which has scope limitation which would be done in case of parallel processing will not show up. 

A Classic indicator for this is that when we load one plant of a material, the Mass Processing is going to pull the entire data and process it.....so when writing back it would also do the same.

You need to relook at the design to consider this and have an alternate solution.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Sending multiple sales / billing documents in one e-mail
entry.content:
Hello,

The scenario described involves consolidating multiple order confirmations or billing documents into a single email. SAP S/4HANA provides functionality that can help in similar scenarios, although specific configurations for email consolidation may need to be manually implemented or custom configured, as there’s no direct built-in setting mentioned in the available documents.

Consolidating Order Confirmations

For order confirmations, SAP’s communication arrangement for email notifications can be configured. Using tools like Output Parameter Determination, you can generate email outputs based on grouping criteria. However, it primarily focuses on sending individual outputs per document unless specifically configured otherwise.

Consolidating Billing Documents

Convergent Billing and Invoice Lists

SAP S/4HANA offers omnichannel convergent billing Sales Billing where you could converge billing data from multiple sources into a combined invoice under specific conditions. This convergent billing can help streamline customer communications.

Solution Billing

Solution Billing allows consolidating billing data Solution Billing from multiple products and services into one combined invoice under solution orders, though this may not directly address separate invoices sent together.

Practical Steps

  • Output Parameter Determination: Use the Output Parameter Determination app How to Configure Output Parameter Determination to define business rules that consolidate the output formats or send multiple attachments in a single email.
  • Custom Implementation: You might need to implement custom code to aggregate and send attachments altogether.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Add Application Autoscaler Service Binding To Deployed Application For High Concurrency Situation
entry.content:
Background Introduction

In some situation, customers find existing application can meet their requirements functionally. But customers have concerns about the performance. For example, we have build mission Add Printing Labels to Your User Experience for SAP S/4HANA Cloud with CAP Node.js, but customers have concerns for high concurrency situation. Then does customer need to adjust the existing code? The answer is no need. Customer can add Application Autoscaler Service Binding To Deployed Application and create Scaling Policy for the deployed application.

Steps:

Step 1. Add Application Autoscaler Service Binding To Deployed Backend Application

Go to your BTP subaccount Cloud Foundry Space in which the backend application has been deployed. Find the backend application.

The following Json file defines the Scaling Policy. I will explain it a little later .

{
  "instance_min_count": 1,
  "instance_max_count": 5,
  "scaling_rules": [
    {
      "metric_type": "memoryused",
      "breach_duration_secs": 600,
      "threshold": 90,
      "operator": ">=",
      "cool_down_secs": 300,
      "adjustment": "+1"
    },
    {
      "metric_type": "memoryused",
      "breach_duration_secs": 600,
      "threshold": 30,
      "operator": "<",
      "cool_down_secs": 300,
      "adjustment": "-1"
    }
  ]
}

Step 2. Restart your backend application.

Step 3. You can check your scaler history in Application Autoscaler Dashboard.

Explanation for Scaling Policy

You can define a policy to scale your application instances either dynamically or based on schedule.

If you want to scale your application instances based on memory or CPU usage, response time, throughput, or custom metrics. You can use Dynamic Scaling Policy

If you want scale your application instances based on schedules, you can use Schedule-Based Scaling Policy.

 

The Ends!

Thanks for your time!

Jacky Liu

entry.title: Re: How to tackle code adaptations for S/4HANA for IS-Hea...
entry.content:
Hi @BaerbelWinkler ,

My solution was as follows:

- if possible find a standard replacement, either for FM or message classes
- if not possible, then create Z-objects for it.

For message classes I was always searching the for the text, and I think I always found a replacement.
For missing FM/classes it was more tricky sometimes, as sometimes it's not simple class or FM to display something, but with the calculations. For most of them I found easily a replacement for the missing FM. Where we haven't found it, we have started deeper checks, what has changed that the FM is missing. In we cases we just omitted the part or we had to adapt our logic in program to follow new process/new way of SAP. 
But wherever possible I reused the exisitng objects. 

I am sure there are plenty of FM and classes to display log and to show the popup to confirm. FORMAT_DATE_4_OUTPUT I don't know, but date formating can be also done in string expression.

 

Cheers
Łukasz

entry.title: SFC-Based Dispatching in REO: Granular Production Scheduling and Process Industry Support
entry.content:
Introduction

As manufacturers look for agility and responsiveness, the ability to adapt production schedules in real-time becomes critical.

SAP Digital Manufacturing’s 'SFC-Based Dispatching' capability in Resource Orchestration (REO) is a step forward in enabling manufacturers to dynamically control production at the most granular level, the Shop Floor Control (SFC) unit.

It is also the first step on enabling proper Process Industries support in REO (MVP, Limited Release Feature), and handling its complexities, see details further down below.

What is SFC-Based Dispatching?

So far, Resource Orchestration (REO) scheduling has been done at the operation/phase level, where individual operations or phases are dispatched to Resources or Work Centers (Production Lines). While effective, this less granular approach can have limitations—especially when considering resource capacities, handling exceptions, or responding to urgent production changes.

SFC-Based Dispatching shifts the focus from scheduling operations or phases to scheduling more granular production units: SFCs or Charges. This enables more refined scheduling and resource allocation, enhancing overall operational flexibility.

In simple terms, this means each part or batch in an order gets its own path through the factory. Instead of treating 100 'quantities' as one large batch for a given operation, the system can create separate schedules for each SFC or Charge and dispatch each one to the appropriate resource or work center at the right time.

See a visual example below:

Business Benefits

Here are some of the main businesses benefits:

Enhanced Production Flexibility

With SFC-level scheduling, you can dispatch specific units based on real-time conditions, such as resource capacity, material availability, machine uptime, or priority shifts. This is ideal for:

- Make-to-Order or highly customized products
- Frequent design or routing changes
- Dynamic environments with variable capacities and workloads

Improved Resource Utilization

By aligning individual SFCs with optimal resources, manufacturers can maximize throughput without overloading any specific work center.

Visibility and Control

In Dispatching and Monitoring app, planners and supervisors can see a clear differentiation between order-based and SFC-based schedules, helping teams focus on execution rather than coordination.

Tight Execution / POD Integration

Production Operator Dashboard (POD) execution is in sync with REO, as SFC scheduled can only show up and be executed in the planned resource, if the system configured that way.

How to Get Started

Enabling SFC-based scheduling requires minimal configuration, see SFC-Based Dispatching in Resource Orchestration for more details.

Process Industry Use Case

With SFC-Based Dispatching, Resource Orchestration (REO) support for Process Orders is officially introduced as a minimum viable product (MVP), and will keep evolving in the next couple of releases, so stay tuned in the next couple of releases!

 
Currently, this is how the flow looks like for Process Orders:
  • Partial Order Release

Once process orders are received from ERP, they can be partially released, creating individual SFCs (Charges) for specific batches with different quantities. 

  • SFC Schedules

Then, individual SFC/Charge schedules can be created, so these SFCs can then be scheduled independently to different resources.

  • SFC-Based Scheduling

Schedule individual SFCs (Charges) to Resources using existing scheduling capabilities and logic, which will ideally dispatch individual SFCs (Charges) to the optimum resources based on capacity, load and other constraints.

In Dispatching and Monitoring app, planners and supervisors can see a clear differentiation between order-based and SFC-based schedules, which are clearly marked by the green borders.

  • In Sync with Execution

Production Operator Dashboard (POD) execution is tightly integrated and in sync with REO, as SFC scheduled can only show up and be executed in the planned resource, if the system configured that way.

 
* See SFC-Based Dispatching in Resource Orchestration for more details.
* Keep in mind that Resource Orchestration (REO) support for Process Orders is introduced as a minimum viable product (MVP), so several restrictions apply.

Conclusion

SFC-Based Dispatching is not just a technical enhancement it’s a strategic tool. It allows manufacturers to shift from rigid planning cycles to agile execution, and is the very first step of enabling REO for Process Industries and its complexities. For production planners and supervisors, this feature offers a clear path to smarter, more adaptive manufacturing.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: API to get PDF output form from sales order
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently, there is no direct API available to retrieve PDF output forms for sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition to be used in SAP Commerce Cloud integration . However, there are a few alternative approaches you can consider:

  1. EDI Capabilities: You can export sales order documents using the EDI capabilities in Output Management (Scope Item - 1LQ) . This method allows you to export the document data, which can then be used to generate PDFs.

  2. Future API Development: SAP is aware of the need for this functionality. There is an ongoing feature request for an API to get PDFs of Sales Orders . You can track updates and vote to increase its priority for development on the SAP Influence portal.

  3. Billing Document API: While not directly applicable to sales orders, the API for Billing Documents does provide PDF retrieval functionality . This could be useful for related processes in your integration.

  4. Alternative Integration Methods: Consider exploring other integration methods between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Commerce Cloud that might allow for document data transfer, which could then be used to generate PDFs on the Commerce Cloud side.

  5. Custom Development: As a last resort, you might need to explore custom development options to bridge this gap, potentially involving side-by-side extensions or custom APIs.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: MIRO changing the vendor as it is "posted"
entry.content:
Hi @MPM124 
In MIRO transaction by default vendor copies from purchase order. If any partner function (payment vendor) exists, then that particular vendor automatically copies to MIRO screen.
Also If po has freight condition types (which are linked to freight vendors), then while posting Invoice for freight conditions, that particular vendors gets copied to MIRO screen.
Above are the standard SAP behavior.
If the Issue is happening for only particular user. we may modify the warning message to Error for M8 - 286 message for that user only. (SPRO > MM > LIV > Define attr. syst. msgs)
If Invoicing vendor changing functionality not using by your client, you may put the error message without any user name in the above configuration path.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#GDTD Is there a method for batch sales order cos...
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, there is a way to manage costing for multiple materials efficiently in SAP systems. You can make use of Mass Costing - Sales Documents Inventory Accounting - Make-to-Order (MTO) Sales Order Costing functionality which allows for the batch processing of sales order costing. This way, costing can be performed for multiple materials at once, streamlining the process and saving time.

Steps for Mass Costing:

  1. Create Sales Orders: This is done using the Create Sales Orders app.
  2. Costing Data: During or after creation, you can choose ‘Menu -> Extras -> Costing’ to start costing.
  3. Batch Processing: Enable batch processing by using relevant mass costing or scheduling apps.
  4. Save Costing Results: Choose to mark your costing data during creation or later through the Change Sales Documents app.

Benefits:

  • Time Efficiency: Batch processing reduces the manual overhead and time taken to cost each individual material.
  • Consistency: Ensure consistent application of costing variants and valuation during the costing process.

Important Notes:

  • Make sure that all materials linked to requirement classes such as S46, S45, S40, S60, are used.
  • The costing variant PPC4 will be used by default and cannot be changed during order creation.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Issue with I_ADDRESS_2 CDS View – No Data Available
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

Today I was asked the following:

 

The standard CDS view I_ADDRESS_2 to fetch detailed address information. Although this CDS view is released and available for use, I am unable to retrieve any data from it.

 

Could you please advise if any additional configuration or activation steps are required for this CDS view to function correctly?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with I_ADDRESS_2 CDS View – No Data Available
entry.content:
Please carefully follow the following steps:

Use the CDS View: I_ADDRESS_2 which is released for the customers. 

I_Address_2 and I_Address both have DCL authorizations built on them, which means to view the data, we need to add ‘With privileged access’ in the SQL statement which is generated in ADT on executing the view. It is not possible to preview the data in the Custom CDS view app.

You should login to the ADT tool (ABAP Development Tools) to execute the CDS view I_ADDRESS_2 

  1. Open the ABAP Development Tools app
  2. File->New->ABAP Project
  3. Currently logged into XYZ (XYZ represents the SAP internal test system)
  4. Select the icon Open ABAP Development Object
  5. Select the Data Definition for I_ADDRESS_2
  6. Observe the below:

    ========================
    @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #PRIVILEGED_ONLY
    @analytics : {
              dataCategory: #DIMENSION,
              dataExtraction: {
               enabled: true }
               }
    @AccessControl.privilegedAssociations: ['_OrganizationAddress', '_PersonAddress', '_EmailAddress', '_CurrentDfltEmailAddress', '_PhoneNumber', '_CurrentDfltLandlinePhoneNmbr', '_CurrentDfltMobilePhoneNumber', '_FaxNumber', '_CurrentDfltFaxNumber', '_UniformResourceIdentifier', '_MainWebsiteURL']
    @EndUserText.label: 'Address of an Organization or a Person'

    ========================

  7. Select F8
  8. On the right side, click on the tab SQL Console
  9. Scroll down to the bottom of the code lines, make the required changes as below

    FROM
      I_ADDRESS_2
      WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
     
  10. Select the button Run
  11. Check the Data Preview on the right side
  12. Able to see the data available in the street fields for the Business Partner

       I_ADDRESS_2~STREETNAME,
       I_ADDRESS_2~STREETPREFIXNAME1,
       I_ADDRESS_2~STREETPREFIXNAME2,
       I_ADDRESS_2~STREETSUFFIXNAME1,
       I_ADDRESS_2~STREETSUFFIXNAME2

There is also a KBA released:

0003387665 - CDS View I_ADDRESS_2 Does Not Display the Data of the Business Partner

entry.title: Re: Delivery should not be Invoiced
entry.content:
Hello,

When using the SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s scope item 5MQ Sell from Stock with Valuated Stock in Transit (5MQ) (“Sell from Stock with Valuated Stock in Transit”), you may encounter a scenario where an invoice is created even though the Actual Date in the Delivery header was not specified. To prevent this, you can employ a few strategies within the system settings.

Strategies to Limit Invoice Creation

Input Validation

  1. Configuration Settings:

    Adjust your configuration settings to enforce a validation rule that requires the Actual Date to be filled before allowing billing. This might involve setting up user exit or enhancement points to check the delivery document before posting.

  2. Document Flow Control:

    Configure the document flow to mandate that certain fields must be populated for document progression. This can include setting the Actual Date as a mandatory field in the delivery process.

Manual Checks

  1. Pre-Posting Checks:

    Implement manual verification steps where warehouse clerks must confirm that all required fields, including Actual Date, are populated before a delivery document is posted.

  2. Status Management:

    Use delivery status management to prevent the transition to billing unless all mandatory fields are complete. Consider adding a “Pending” status that acts as a cue for missing information.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: SAC: Data Action to Enrich Information
entry.content:
@IoanaZamfir 

I think you can drive the company and cost center by multiplying the bottom records gehaltskomponent 1000 records with FTE records. 

Something like below

VARIABLEMEMBER #CO of [d/company]

VARIABLEMEMBER #CC of [d/costcentre]

DATA(d/company]=#CO,[d/costcentre]=#CC)=RESULTLOOKUP([d/gehaltskomponent]="1000")

DATA([d/gehaltskomponent]="1000)=RESULTLOOKUP(d/company]=#CO,[d/costcentre]=#CC,[d/gehaltskomponent]="1000") * (RESULTLOOKUP(d/gehaltskomponent]="FTE")*0+1)

The part in bold will be result in 1 so that will have no impact on your values. You might have to adjust as per your model other dimensions which are not visible in the table.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Define Custom Email Template Names for Approved/Rejec...
entry.content:
Hello,

To customize email notifications for the approval and rejection of Preliminary Billing Documents in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you need to follow these steps:

Email Templates

  1. Predelivered Email Templates How to Set Up Approval Workflows for Preliminary Billing Documents
    • Approval: You can use the pre-delivered template for approval notification. Copy the template to create a custom one.
    • Rejection: Similarly, a predelivered template for rejection exists and can be copied to create a custom version.

Steps to Configure Templates

  1. Access Maintain Email Templates app

    • Locate the pre-delivered templates relevant to your workflow.
    • Copy the templates to create custom versions.

  2. Template Customization

    • Customize the email body and subject as required. Configuring Email Notification Templates
    • Ensure the IDs of custom templates are correctly configured to replace the default IDs.

Template IDs for Preliminary Billing Documents

  • Approval Notification: The template ID for approval notifications can typically be something along the lines of YY1_APPROVED_EMAIL.
  • Rejection Notification: The template ID for rejection notifications is usually structured similarly, such as YY1_REJECTED_EMAIL.

Please check these IDs in the provided system documentation or by inspecting existing templates in your application. More details on the exact naming conventions can be found in the respective SAP documentation for preliminary billing documents workflows.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Sales quotes or sales order replication from sales cl...
entry.content:
Hello,

To achieve integration between SAP Sales Cloud V2 and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, particularly for replicating sales quotes or sales orders, several steps can be followed using SAP’s integration tools and methodologies:

Steps to Enable Integration

  1. Cloud Connector Reachability: Ensure that the Cloud Connector is set up correctly and is reachable. This acts as a secure connection between SAP Cloud environments and on-premises or other cloud systems.

  2. Utilize Cloud Platform Integration (CPI): Log in to SAP CPI and identify the relevant integration flows (iFlows) needed for sales quote or sales order replication. Deploy these iFlows to manage data transfer effectively. CPI helps bridge the Sales Cloud and S/4HANA Cloud systems with predefined integration scenarios.

  3. Communication Arrangements: In the SAP Sales Cloud, create and maintain the communication arrangements required SAP CLOUD FOR CUSTOMER INTEGRATION WITH S/4 HANA O… for the artifacts you’ve identified. This includes setting up outbound and inbound communication for sales quotes and sales orders.

  4. Configure IDOC Settings in S/4HANA: If IDOC adapter types are used, maintain configurations in transactions like WE20 and WE21 to ensure proper partner profile and port settings. These transactions help manage inbound and outbound IDOCs in S/4HANA Cloud.

  5. Business Process Synchronization: Execute steps in the Sales Cloud to ensure that sales order creation and replication occur successfully between these systems. This may involve steps such as creating sales quotes, requesting external pricing, and submitting for external document creation which in turn creates sales orders within S/4HANA.

  6. Monitor and Test: Regularly monitor the integration flows and test the setup to verify successful replication of sales documents and troubleshoot any issues that arise.

This approach combines cloud integration mechanisms with IDOCs and communication arrangements to sync and manage sales documents across the SAP Cloud landscape, ensuring seamless integration between the systems.

Resources

  • Cloud Interface Tools: Utilize SAP’s Cloud Platform Integration and IDOC configurations for direct integration of data between SAP systems.
  • SAP APIs: Where possible, leverage SAP’s API Business Hub for accessing SAP’s APIs, which can provide direct integration capabilities.

This methodology ensures data consistency and connectivity between SAP Sales Cloud V2 and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, irrespective of direct replication constraints noted in the documentation.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Business Partner Country & Language are mandatory in SAP S/4HANA On Premise
entry.content:
Hello @Kaz_Iwamoto 

I know your Blog is quite old, but maybe you have a hint for me anyway...

In Dec. 2024 I did a CVI and everything went fine. All contact persons got created as Business Partner person without any issue.

Unfortuantely S4 transition got postponed so the customer is still working with customer & vendor.

Now the question arises why contact persons cannot be related to a BP, when they are assigned in a vendor. Error message AM216 appears in PPO like described here: 3292825 - Language and Country are always required  ... this one tells "Bear in mind that BPs with category 'person' use the fields "Correspondence Language" and "Language" below Name section" ... and after having maintained the language in the contact person, then the contact person BP is related automatically in the vendor BP.

As to my knowledge there was no change at all in the Customizing ... the field control for role type BUP001 shows country and language as mandatory, but it should have been the same, 6 months ago.

So why could the ~10.000 contact persons all be created as BP and also be related successfully without language field filled, and now it is required? ... have you seen such a case or is it too difficult to judge?

Thanks and BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: STO Process Error
entry.content:
Hello,

Your client is experiencing a calculation error during the STO (Stock Transport Order) process where the system is returning an incorrect multiplication result, affecting the GST value as well.

Suggested Resolution

  1. Adjust Material Price: Since the calculation discrepancy affects subsequent tax computations, using the ‘Change Material Cost’ (MR21) 3376733 - Error C+843 ‘Total valuated inventory amount for currency &2 is too high (&1)’ raised is a viable solution. This app allows you to adjust the price values.

    • Perform smaller incremental price changes to avoid generating large price differences that might result in errors.

  2. Round-Off Settings: Ensure that the material price rounding setting aligns with your client’s financial requirements. Proper rounding can mitigate calculation errors.

  3. SAP Help Documentation: Refer to the SAP Help Documentation for detailed guidance on adjusting pricing and taxation settings.

  4. Check System Configuration: Verify that system configurations regarding currency rounding are correctly set.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Change Category in Applicant Status Configuration
entry.content:
Hi @sureshbandaru83 ,

You're changing the category of a specific onboarding status (likely a final status like "Hired") from the "Hired" category to "Miscellaneous" in Provisioning.

Potential Benefits:

  • Avoiding double counts: If dashboards, reports, or integrations are keyed to the "Hired" category, changing this status to "Miscellaneous" prevents it from being included.
  • Cleaner reporting: Could help differentiate between true hires and onboarding cases that don’t align with your reporting definitions of "hire."

Potential Impacts:

  1. Reporting & Analytics (Ad Hoc Reports / People Analytics / ORD):
    • Any report logic filtering or aggregating by status category will need to be updated to reflect this change.
    • Otherwise, counts may decrease or reports might exclude relevant records unintentionally.                      
  2. Dashboards & Tiles (Homepage Tiles / Story Reports):
    • Any dashboards relying on the "Hired" category for metrics (e.g., new hires, onboarding conversion) will no longer count these statuses.
    • A review of each dashboard or tile configuration is needed to realign logic.                                              
  3. Business Rules or Workflow Logic:
    • Check if any Business Rules are triggered based on the status category or specific status. These rules might not work as intended after the change.                                                                                 
  4. Integrations (e.g., to Payroll or other HR Systems):
    • If downstream systems listen for "Hired" category status to trigger integrations (e.g., provisioning a user in payroll), they may not run unless updated.                                                                                     
  5. Recruiting & Onboarding Process Metrics:                        

Recommend to try in Test environment:

    • Make the category change in Dev or QA and validate end-to-end behavior.
    • Document the original configuration and the reason for the change.
    • Take screenshots of the provisioning configuration for audit purposes.

Hope this helps.

Thanks,

Kr,

Karthick.

entry.title: Re: How to use SOAP API B2B For BOM sales order creation
entry.content:
Hello,

You can indeed use the SOAP API B2B (OrderRequest_In) for inbound sales order creation involving a Bill of Materials (BOM). However, if you are encountering the ‘Pricing procedure cannot be determined’ Trouble shooting for SOAP API Sales Order/Customer… error, it suggests there might be issues related to the configuration or payload data.

Steps to Address the Error

  • Ensure Proper Pricing Procedure Configuration: The system requires that a valid pricing procedure is determined based on Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, and Division specified in the payload. Verify these settings in your configuration and ensure they are correctly mapped in the payload.

  • Payload Validation: Double-check that the payload includes all necessary fields accurately. For BOM material to explore correctly in a sales order, make sure the <SupplierProductID> is mapped accurately to correspond with SAP’s internal material number.

  • Check BOM Configuration: Normally, when creating a sales order via VA01, BOM explosion happens automatically. Ensure that similar configuration settings are applied to the API environment. BOM usage and settings can be verified in SAP’s configuration under BOM-related transactions.

Example Payload Structure

Though not exhaustive, here’s a basic structure of how the payload might look when passing BOM related data:

<OrderItem>
    <PurchaseOrderItemID>10</PurchaseOrderItemID>
    <OrderItemText>Assembly Product</OrderItemText>
    <RequestedQuantity unitCode="PCE">1.0</RequestedQuantity>
    <Product>
        <BuyerProductID>MainProductID</BuyerProductID>
        <SupplierProductID>BOMMaterialSAPNo</SupplierProductID>
    </Product>
    <ScheduleLine>
        <RequestedDeliveryDate>2023-12-01</RequestedDeliveryDate>
        <ScheduleLineOrderQuantity unitCode="PCE">1.0</ScheduleLineOrderQuantity>
    </ScheduleLine>
</OrderItem>

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: SOAP API B2B Inbound Sales Order
entry.content:
Hello,

When creating a sales order, if you’re unable to set a different ship-to party using various identifiers like SAP customer number or external customer number, it could be due to the way partner determination and automatic party assignment are configured in your system.

Steps to Resolve:

  1. Partner Determination Configuration: Ensure that the ship-to party role is correctly configured Working with Partner Determination (Example) in the partner determination procedure for sales documents.

    • Go to the configuration activities such as ‘Set Up Partner Determination for Sales Document Header’ and ‘Set Up Partner Determination for Sales Document Item’. Make sure the ship-to party is configured as changeable.

  2. Modify Customer Master Data:

    • Open the ‘Manage Customer Master Data’ app. How to determine the Ship-to Party automatically from the Sold-to Party when creating a sales order?
    • Check the ‘Sales Areas’ tab and ensure that multiple ship-to parties are assigned to the sold-to party in customer master data. This might help in prompting the selection of the appropriate ship-to party during sales order creation.

  3. Verify Master Data:

    • Ensure that the business partner roles and functions are correctly maintained for the customer in question.
    • Look into the ‘Partner Functions’ tab and add or correct any missing partner roles.

  4. System Prompt for Ship-to Party Selection:

    • If multiple ship-to parties are associated with the sold-to party, use apps that prompt you to select the ship-to party instead of defaulting to one.
    • In the ‘Create Sales Orders - VA01’ app, the system usually prompts you to choose from available ship-to parties.

  5. Check SAP System Behavior: Ensure that the system behavior is as expected During Sales Order creation the Ship-to party is automatically filled with regards to partner determination. Sometimes ignoring system prompts can lead to default settings being applied incorrectly.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh Architecture Patterns
entry.content:
This blog post explores common architectural patterns using SAP Event Mesh in a cloud-native integration landscape.

Audience & Prerequisites

Audience: Integration architects and developers familiar with AMQP principles and SAP Cloud Integration.
Prerequisites: Basic understanding of event-driven design.

Key Components

  • Producer System: Source of events (e.g., e-commerce platform).
  • SAP Cloud Integration Suite: Acts as an AMQP broker or event router.
  • Consumer System(s): Subscribers processing events (e.g., warehouse, analytics).

1. Single Publisher → Single Event Type → Single Subscriber

 

Use Case
A customer places an online order. The e-commerce app publishes an OrderCreated event, and a warehouse service processes it to trigger shipping.

Motivation
Simplest guaranteed-delivery scenario for point-to-point integration.

Description

  • Producer sends events to a dedicated queue.
  • Consumer subscribes to the queue for ordered processing.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Guaranteed delivery, simple error handling.
  • ❌ Cons: Tight coupling, no scalability.

When to Use
Legacy system integrations or one-to-one workflows.

Configuration

  • Queue: Queue_OrderCreated
  • Protocol: AMQP (pull-based).

2. Single Publisher → Single Event Type → Multiple Subscribers (Fan-out)

 

Use Case
A retail platform publishes OrderCreated events to notify analytics, billing, and notification services in parallel.

Motivation
Broadcast events to multiple consumers for independent processing.

Description

  • Producer publishes to a topic.
  • Event Mesh replicates the event to subscriber queues.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Decoupled subscribers, scalable.
  • ❌ Cons: Resource overhead, management complexity.

When to Use
Multiple downstream systems need the same event.

Configuration

  • Topic: Topic_OrderCreated
  • Subscriber Queues: Queue_Analytics, Queue_Billing, Queue_Notification.

3. Multiple Event Types → Single Subscriber

 

Use Case
A fulfillment engine handles OrderCreated and OrderUpdated events from a single subscription.

Motivation
Centralize logic for related event types.

Description

  • Producers emit events to a shared topic.
  • Subscriber filters events by headers (e.g., type=Created).

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Fewer endpoints, unified logic.
  • ❌ Cons: Filtering overhead, load concentration.

When to Use
A service needs multiple event types.

Configuration

  • Topic: Topic_OrderEvents
  • Queue: Queue_OrderProcessor with header-based bindings.

4. Multi-Topic Selective Subscription

 

Use Case
A procurement system subscribes to material/vendor events, while analytics uses product events.

Motivation
Domain-driven subscriptions for segregated data.

Description

  • Producers publish to domain-specific topics.
  • Consumers bind to relevant topics.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Fine-grained control, domain isolation.
  • ❌ Cons: Binding management.

When to Use
Segregated event types per domain.

Configuration

  • Topics: Topic_MaterialEvents, Topic_ProductEvents, Topic_VendorEvents.
  • Queues: Queue_Procurement (material/vendor), Queue_Analytics (product).

5. Aggregator Pattern

 

Use Case
An integration flow aggregates daily material/product updates into a MasterDataBundle for reporting.

Motivation
Batch processing of related events.

Description

  • SAP Cloud Integration (CI) consumes source queues, aggregates data, and publishes a bundled event.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Simplified downstream consumption.
  • ❌ Cons: Latency, complex logic.

When to Use
Batch reporting or composite events.

Configuration

  • Source Queues: Queue_MaterialEvents, Queue_ProductEvents.
  • Output Topic: Topic_MasterDataBundle.

6. Event Enrichment Pipeline

 

Use Case
Enrich raw order events with customer credit scores and product availability.

Motivation
Decouple enrichment steps from producers/consumers.

Description

  • CI flows progressively enrich events across stages.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Modular design, separation of concerns.
  • ❌ Cons: Increased latency.

When to Use
Progressive event enhancement.

Configuration

  • Topics: Topic_RawOrderEvents → Topic_EnrichedEvents.
  • Queues: Queue_Enrich1, Queue_Billing, Queue_Shipping.

7. Scatter–Gather Pattern

 

Use Case
A credit-check flow queries two agencies in parallel and aggregates responses.

Motivation
Parallel execution and result collation.

Description

  • CI publishes requests to a topic.
  • Subscribers process requests and publish responses.
  • Aggregator CI flow correlates responses.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Parallelism, resilience.
  • ❌ Cons: Correlation complexity.

When to Use
Multi-service orchestration.

Configuration

  • Topics: Topic_Requests, Topic_Responses.
  • Queues: Queue_ServiceA, Queue_ServiceB, Queue_Aggregator.

8. Error Handling & Dead-Letter Queue

 

Use Case
Malformed events are moved to DLQ_OrderProcessor after retries.

Motivation
Isolate poison messages for analysis.

Description

  • Configure queues to route failed messages to a DLQ.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: Prevents message loss.
  • ❌ Cons: Requires monitoring.

When to Use
Critical pipelines needing reliability.

Configuration

  • Primary Queue: Queue_OrderProcessor.
  • DLQ: DLQ_OrderProcessor (bound via dead-letter exchange).

9. Webhook Integration Pattern

 

Use Case
Event Mesh invokes a third-party SMS gateway’s webhook on OrderShipped events.

Motivation
Real-time integration with HTTP endpoints.

Description

  • Event Mesh pushes events to HTTP endpoints via POST.

Pros & Cons

  • ✅ Pros: No persistent consumer needed.
  • ❌ Cons: HTTP reliability challenges.

When to Use
Lightweight integrations with REST APIs.

Configuration

  • Topic: Topic_OrderNotifications.
  • Webhook: https://api.sms-gateway.com/notify (TLS + OAuth).

Conclusion

Choose patterns based on coupling, scalability, and latency needs. SAP Event Mesh’s flexibility with queues, topics, and webhooks supports diverse integration scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Generating Billing Document Request from Service Cont...
entry.content:
Hello,

When generating Billing Document Requests (BDRs) from a service contract in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and encountering separate BDRs for each line item despite sharing the same billing date, you can address this through the custom configuration and the use of specific features.

Steps to Achieve This

  1. Cloud Business Add-in (BAdI):

    • Introduce a custom logic using BAdI fields to handle and modify how BDR items are grouped. You can modify fields such as settlement dates or billing dates to programmatically force integration into a singular BDR.

  2. Configure Aggregation Criteria through CDS Views: How to Consume CDS View I_BillingRequestItem - Business Case: Adding Service Contract Dates to Invoice Item

    • Consider consuming relevant CDS Views like I_BillingRequestItem to understand or manipulate billing request lines.
    • By extending fields in the CDS view, you can group based on custom field logic rather than relying purely on standard attributes.

  3. Utilize the Schedule Billing Document Request Creation App: Parallel Processing of Scheduling BDR Creation - SAP Community

    • Review and adjust app settings for batch creation processes ensuring aggregation based on shared criteria.

  4. Simulation for Testing:

    • Conduct parallel processing simulations to ensure batch job parameters align with producing consolidated BDRs.

  5. Exploration of Parallel Processing Capabilities:

    • Maximize parallel processing parameters in the system, enabling the integration that may streamline single BDR generation.

  6. Utilize Help Resources:

    • Utilize the SAP Help Portal for detailed guides and support documents related to BDR creation settings.

These configurations can typically align line items into singular BDR entries based on similar characteristics like billing dates, which should resolve the issue effectively.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Overall Open Orders
entry.content:
Hello,

The overall open orders calculation includes both open orders and open billing requests. However, there might be a misunderstanding on how partially invoiced lines are handled in this calculation.

  • Open Billing Sales - Key Figures Based on Delivery Documents Requests: Refers to sales documents that are relevant for invoicing but have not been fully invoiced. It may include certain debit memo requests or sales orders with billing plans that have not yet led to customer invoice creation.

  • Partial Invoicing: If invoice lines are partially shipped or invoiced, standard behavior may still consider them under open billing requests until they are fully invoiced.

To ensure the report reflects actual open status correctly, verify if the customization settings in the billing plan or relevant configuration might address:

  1. Billing Plan Setup: Ensure the billing plan correctly allocates invoices upon partial invoicing to adjust the open amount.

  2. Business Rules for Billing: Examine any custom business rules that handle invoicing status updates in case of partial shipments or invoices.

  3. Report Customization: Use custom reports or views to refine how open billing requests are calculated, incorporating logic that detects and adjusts for partial invoicing.

If these options are configured correctly, you can ensure that partially invoiced lines do not mistakenly remain marked as ‘fully open,’ thereby improving accuracy in reporting overall open order status.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Update Sales Order Price with Supplier Invoice Price...
entry.content:
Hello,

In a ‘bill-through’ scenario where the supplier invoice amount differs from the original purchase order price, SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides features to handle price updates automatically. Using SAP Build Process Automation, you can set up workflows that trigger price updates on sales orders based on the supplier invoice discrepancies.

Steps to Implement

  1. Enable Situational Handling: Utilize SAP S/4HANA’s intelligent Situation Handling Situation Handling â€“ Inform, Insight and Decide - SAP Community to set up alerts when discrepancies are detected between the supplier invoice and purchase order price.

  2. SAP Build Process Automation: Leverage SAP Build Process Automation Harnessing the Potential of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Pub… - SAP Community to create a workflow that can read supplier invoice details and automatically update the pricing in the sales order.

    • Use APIs such as ‘Condition Record for Pricing in Sales’ to update the pricing conditions.

  3. Configuration in SAP S/4HANA:

    • Ensure pricing procedures are adequately defined to react to changes in procurement costs.
    • Set up communication scenarios for seamless data flow between different systems via SAP’s Integration Suite.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Writing Characteristic Values to New Documents - ECTR
entry.content:
I'm attempting to use the SAP Engineering Control Center connector API to set the characteristics of a document. I've gotten as far as the below snippet for reading those classification just fine, but when it comes time to write characteristics back to a document record, I've hit a brick wall

All of the methods found in the documention that could handle this seem to be deprecated. What is the current recommened method for modifying an existing documents classification OR creating a new document and setting those characteristics at creation?

 

 public static void readProperties(IPlmConnector conn, string docNum, string docType, string docStatus)
        {
        //Initialize The Search for the input document info....
            DocSearch docSearch = new DocSearch();
            docSearch.DocNumber = docNum;
            docSearch.DocType = docType;
            docSearch.DocStatus = docStatus;
            //docSearch.LoadWithComponents = true;
            //Query ECTR for the Document
            PlmRequestPartList ListParts = new PlmRequestPartList();
            IPlmResponse plmResponse = conn.SearchDocument(ListParts, docSearch, Com.Dscsag.Plm.Comm.SelectionMode.MULTI);
            string partFileName = plmResponse.Parts[0].Filename;

        //Among the results, the most recently created file will filter to the top, so Part[0] is almost always the desired option.
            IPlmRequestPart part = new
            PlmRequestPartImpl(partFileName);
            PlmRequestPartList parts = new PlmRequestPartList();
            parts.Add(part);
            IPlmResponse response = conn.ProvideInfo(parts, true, false, true);
            PlmResponsePartList provideInfoParts = response.Parts;
        //Extract Data From the Part
            foreach (IPlmResponsePart responsePart in provideInfoParts)
            {
                PartInfo info = responsePart.PartInfo;
               

                if (responsePart.Action == PartAction.DO_NOTHING && info != null)
                {
                    Console.WriteLine("Results of Provide Info Process for: " + responsePart.Filename);
                    Console.WriteLine("Param: IS_OK: " + info.Ok);
                    if (info.Ok)
                    {
                        // trace description
                        string partDescription;
                        info.Metadata.TryGetValue("DESCRIPTION", out partDescription);
                        Console.WriteLine("Descripton: " + partDescription);
                        // trace all meta data
                        Console.WriteLine("MetaData...");
                        foreach (var entry in info.Metadata)
                            Console.WriteLine(" NAME: " + entry.Key + " VALUE:" + entry.Value);
                        foreach (var entry in info.Classification[0].Chars)
                            Console.WriteLine(" NAME: " + entry.Name + " VALUE:" + entry.Value);

                        Console.WriteLine("Descripton: " + partDescription);
                    }
                }
            }
        }

entry.title: Re: RAP: Unable Create multiple Outbound Delivery Splits...
entry.content:
Hello,

Developing a RAP application for creating multiple outbound deliveries from a single sales order with one item can indeed involve several technical challenges. Here are some steps and considerations that might help you:

Challenges and Possible Solutions

1. Using BAPI ‘BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CREATE_SLS’ within Unmanaged Methods SAP® Digital Supply Chain Logistics in SAP S/4HANA

  • Problem: Unable to use BAPI within unmanaged Create or Save methods.
  • Solution: Ensure that the correct destination is specified. If ‘DESTINATION NONE’ is not working, verify the system and connection configurations. Consider creating a wrapper function module around the BAPI to handle errors more gracefully.

2. Passing Specific Delivery Quantity

  • Problem: Unable to pass specific delivery quantity to Business Object ‘I_OutboundDeliveryTP’ Create Method.
  • Solution: You may need to map the quantity explicitly within the RAP framework. Delivery Management - Creating Multiple Outbound Deliveries Ensure that your method parameters are set up correctly to accept and utilize the quantity value. Validate the input data and ensure it is accessible to the Create Method.

3. Creating Multiple Deliveries

  • Problem: Unable to create multiple outbound deliveries within one unmanaged Create/save Method.
  • Solution: Consider looping through the required quantity split logic within your method. You can implement a batch processing technique or multiple API calls to handle separate creation transactions for each delivery.

Recommendations

  • Testing & Debugging: Use tracing and debugging tools to identify where failures occur in the RAP framework.
  • Error Handling: Implement robust error handling in your RAP methods to catch and resolve issues. This may include logging and returning informative error messages.
  • SAP RAP Documentation: Refer to SAP’s official documentation and community for guidelines specific to RAP architecture. They may have examples or discussion threads that address similar issues.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: BSP future in Private Cloud 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
Hi SAP Gurus,

I'm just after a bit of advice and guidance regarding future state of BSPs in a Private Cloud S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 scenario.

Now the general guidance from SAP is to modernise BSPs and older UIs to Fiori apps or potentially SAP Build Apps, and I fully agree and endorse that, but our organisation has been given specific guidance that BSPs will no longer be supported in S/4HANA.

Now, I know that BSPs deployed in our PoC S/4HANA environment function perfectly fine, and that as part of the ABAP stack they currently should continue to function as intended.  BSPs can also be applied to a tile as a URL link and launched from the Fiori Launchpad.  That launchpad when federated to Work Zone SE also functions as expected.

Problem is I've not been able to locate any depreciation or sunset notification or notes (with the exception of CL_BSP_LOGIN_APPLICATION), that support the statement that BSPs will not be supported in S/4HANA, so I am after some advice from the community in case I've missed something, as I cannot find any withdrawal of support from SAP for this aging UI technology.  

Thanks in advance for any responses.

entry.title: Re: Warning "Data has been changed in parallel session" w...
entry.content:
Hello @t_jungesblut 

As the note 3440905 - Warning "Data has been changed in parallel session" when save Freight Order that is a standard functionality. Thus it is not an issue to be resolved. That is simply how deferred processing in TM works.

If you don't like it, you can minimize the effect by not using creation/change/save strategies. They are customize in on freight order type level - SPRO: Transportation Management -> Freight Order Management -> Freight Order -> Define Freight Order Types.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Become a Certified SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Project Manager
entry.content:
In the dynamic world of digital transformation, where businesses are constantly evolving to stay competitive, project management is more crucial than ever. Central to this landscape is the role of professionals equipped to manage complex cloud projects. With the newly launched SAP Certification for Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects (C_S4PM), you can now become a certified project manager and take charge of leading transformative initiatives.

Designed for Project Managers, Consultants, and Technical Leads, this certification empowers you to excel in managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations, positioning you as a key player in your organization's digital transformation projects with strategic guidance and expert integration.

How can you prepare for this certification?

You can prepare for the exam in your own pace, by completing the "Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects" learning journey, designed to provide comprehensive insight into SAP S/4HANA Cloud project management. With free access to the learning journey, you can deepen your expertise of managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations, specifically focusing on SAP tools, methodologies, and SAP Business Suite portfolio.

Additionally, SAP Community blog posts provide some supporting information to deepen your understanding and preparation:

  • "Guiding Your Cloud Journey: The New SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Adoption Plan Roadmap"
  • "SAP Partner: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Implementation Tools Overview"
  • "News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and more"

You can also explore more insights about SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition on SAP for Me and taking a look at the release notes.

As a prerequisite, candidates should possess project management experience, preferred in ERP implementation, especially SAP Cloud-related projects. Additionally, hands-on experience with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solutions and experience with managing cloud-based SAP projects is beneficial.

Kickstart your journey today

By becoming a certified project manager for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you will be able to position yourself as a key contributor to your organization's growth and innovation. You can demonstrate a high level of competency in orchestrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition projects, showcasing expertise and leadership in cloud initiatives that are aligned with business objectives.

Certified learners need to continuously keep their skills up-to-date and certifications valid by completing mandatory assessments before their certification expires. With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can prepare for and take the stay certified assessments at any time during the validity period of your certification.  If you want to know more on how to get and stay certified, you can read this blog post.

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and get inspired by reading learner stories on SAP Community, ask questions and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: COMING SOON! Product Compliance Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

Topics in this Academic Lesson include: regional compliance regulations, the costs of non-compliance, the impact of product compliance issues on business processes, product compliance throughout the value chain, and systems controls related to compliance for the organization, its customers and its suppliers. 

The first Activity of this Academic Lesson provides a comprehensive explanation of the various facets of product compliance management; such as, chemical compliance, dangerous goods management, data sheets and documentation, and product compliance-related analytics. The second Activity provides students with the opportunity to complete product compliance tasks directly in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Business, product design and development, engineering, manufacturing and production, transportation management, data management, and studies relevant to oil, gas, and chemical industries.

Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university.  

DOWNLOAD:

Microsoft Word editable format: coming soon

PDF non-editable format:  coming soon

entry.title: Re: Upload General Journal Entry - Incomplete documents
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

When using the "Upload General Journal Entries" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the standard template, the system will post the valid entries from the file and skip any that contain errors. This partial posting is actually the expected system behavior, and unfortunately, there's no built-in setting to make the upload act like an “all-or-nothing” process (where nothing gets posted if any entry is wrong).

Here’s a practical workaround :

Use the "Import General Journal Entries" app instead

This app works a bit differently and gives you more control. You can upload the file, simulate the entries first to catch any issues, and only post the file when everything looks good. That way, you can make sure nothing gets posted unless the entire file is error-free.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Why is the COGS Third Party 5030200 standard YCOA acc...
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

SAP sets the 5030200 COGS Third Party account and most 5* accounts  as not tax relevant by default because these accounts are used for internal cost reporting, not for transactions where VAT is actually calculated.

Tax is handled when you post an invoice (e.g., a vendor invoice for goods), not when the cost hits the COGS account. COGS is usually updated automatically after goods are delivered, so there's no tax to post again at that point.

Marking these accounts as non-tax relevant helps prevent mistakes, keeps the tax logic clean and compliant, and ensures tax is only posted where it’s legally required like on sales or purchase transactions.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Update May 2025

The May-update of the Partner Reference Application “Poetry Slam Manager” addresses application management topics as well as architecture enhancements:

  • The ability to adopt customer specific expectations in multitenant standard solutions is key for market success. The chapter Enable Key User Flexibility Using SAP Build Work Zone provides you all information at hand to empower your customers to customize the UIs of multitenant applications to meet company-specific preferences. 
  • We added the SAP Job Scheduling Service to schedule and trigger jobs for background execution in chapter Schedule Jobs and Add Background Execution. The example provides a reference architecture to benefit from background jobs as partner application manager, as well as customer business user.

  • The tutorial SAP Business One Integration Using a Cloud Connector describes how to establish a secure connection between SAP BTP-based applications and on-premises solutions such as SAP Business One and SAP S/4HANA using the SAP Connectivity Service and the SAP Cloud Connector.
  • For application managers we added tutorials and configuration examples to upgrade multitenant SaaS applications and CI/CD pipelines using the SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery Service.

Our next stop to meet you in person is the Code Connect event re>≡CAP 2025 on July 9th at the SAP University in St. Leon-Rot, Germany. Looking forward to meet you there! 😊
